violations.text: Employee observed eating while preparing food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (mandolin and vegetable peeler) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Imitation crab was observed held at 54 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Discussed placing food in pans actually inside the unit, not on top of covered containers. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Chicken was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Discussed additional walk in freezer space with AVI manager. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the ( ) area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023

Orange Chicken bowls were observed held at 124-129 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Orange Chicken was voluntarily disposed of by the PIC. Rice was reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023

Sushi was observed held at 60 degrees Fahrenheit in open air case for retail. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was removed for sale to cool in freezer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023

Cooked Broccoli and Carrots were observed held at 67 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per staff, food was cooked around 9:30 AM this day. Foods were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023

Sushi was observed held at 50-59 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Sushi was placed into freezer to blast chill food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023

ROP Tuna was observed with packaging still intact. ROP seafood must be opened prior to thawing. Tuna temping at 38 degrees Fahrenheit, packaging opened during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023

The presence of small flies is not controlled as evidenced by live insects observed during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3 bay sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the dry storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023
